_meta.title: Peter Lynch Tenbagger Fast Grower — Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Peter Lynch (One Up on Wall Street)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all vectorized hyper-growth velocity thresholds, PEG corridors, and pre-discovery filters inside Project Beast (Framework 5: fw_lynch). 4 materialized binary pillar flags drive a 0-4 score. v1.1 adds EPS per share gate, FCF cash verification, FII+DII combined institutional threshold, promoter buying signal, and inventory surge disqualifier.
_meta.provenance_note: Every threshold in this specification is taken from Lynch's 6-category system (Ch.3, pp.96-114), the PEG framework (Ch.13, p.183), the institutional ownership rule (Ch.9, p.122), and the India adaptation companion (Chapters 2, 7, 8, 9). v1.1 book-grounded improvements: EPS per share from Ch15 (dilution-adjusted), FCF gate from Ch13 p196, FII+DII from Ch9/Ch15 ('institutional ownership'), promoter buying from Ch15 p208+213, inventory surge from Ch13 p197. Qualitative criteria (boring name, depressing business, no analyst coverage) require scuttlebutt that cannot be in a CSV and are intentionally excluded. All calculations run as loop-free, vectorized array masks with conservative NaN handling.

unit_notes.eps_gr_5y: PERCENTAGE — 15.0 = 15% 5-year EPS CAGR. v1.1 primary earnings gate (dilution-adjusted per-share). 3Y fallback via eps_gr_3y when 5Y unavailable.
unit_notes.eps_gr_3y: PERCENTAGE — 15.0 = 15% 3-year EPS CAGR. v1.1 fallback when eps_gr_5y is unavailable.
unit_notes.pat_gr_3y: PERCENTAGE — v1.0 legacy: 15.0 = 15% 3-year PAT CAGR. Replaced in v1.1 by eps_gr_5y (EPS per share, dilution-adjusted). Retained in unit_notes for backward reference.
unit_notes.free_cash_flow: ABSOLUTE INR Cr — v1.1 cash gate: free_cash_flow > 0 confirms Lynch Ch13 p196 'make sure it is free cash flow'. Fallback: fcf_yield > 0.
unit_notes.fcf_yield: PERCENTAGE — v1.1 cash gate fallback: fcf_yield > 0. Used when free_cash_flow is unavailable.
unit_notes.peg: FLOAT RATIO — 0.75 = 0.75x PEG (P/E divided by EPS CAGR). Lynch: 'A P/E of 30 for a company growing at 40% (PEG 0.75) represents a deep bargain.'
unit_notes.fii_holdings: PERCENTAGE — v1.1: combined FII+DII < 20% threshold. fillna(50): if FII data missing, assume already discovered.
unit_notes.dii_holdings: PERCENTAGE — v1.1 addition: combined FII+DII < 20% threshold. fillna(50): if DII data missing, assume already discovered. Lynch Ch15: 'institutional ownership' means all institutions.
unit_notes.debt_to_equity: FLOAT RATIO — 0.5 = 0.5x D/E. Lynch: 'Companies without debt don't go bankrupt.' Companion Ch.8: D/E < 0.25 is preferred; screener threshold D/E < 0.5 (Ch.9). Gate is STRICT '<' (exactly 0.5 FAILS).
unit_notes.promoter_holdings: PERCENTAGE — 45.0 = 45% promoter/founder ownership. v1.1 OR condition: promoter >= 45% OR change_promoter_1y > 0 (actively buying).
unit_notes.change_promoter_1y: PERCENTAGE — v1.1 addition: promoter stake change over 1Y. > 0 means promoter is actively buying. Lynch Ch15 p208+213: 'insider buying is a positive sign.' fillna(-1): NaN assumed not buying.
unit_notes.inv_growth: PERCENTAGE — v1.1 disqualifier: inventory growth rate (pre-computed in data_engine.py). Lynch Ch13 p197: 'when inventories grow faster than sales, it's a red flag.' Vetos lynch_pass but does NOT reduce lynch_score.

pillar_v_growth_velocity._book_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.3 p.104: 'small, aggressive new enterprises that grow at 20 to 25 percent a year.' Ch.13 p.183: earnings growth is the primary driver of stock returns. Ch.13 p.196: 'Before you invest in a growth company, make sure that it's free cash flow — what's left after normal capital spending — and not just earnings.' Ch.15 p.212: 'the earnings growth rate' (Lynch uses EPS per share, not total PAT — dilution-adjusted). Companion Ch.7 India Filter: Revenue CAGR 3Y > 20%.
pillar_v_growth_velocity._display_label: Hyper-Growth Runway: Revenue 5Y >= 20% AND EPS per share >= 15% AND positive FCF

revenue_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN revenue CAGR → 0 < 20 → gate fails (cannot verify Fast Grower status without growth data).
revenue_gate.rationale: Lynch's primary category definition: a Fast Grower must compound revenue at 20%+ annually. Below 20% = Stalwart or Slow Grower — different category, different valuation framework, different holding period. Lynch warns against anything above 25% because hyper-speculative stories at 50%+ growth usually end badly.

earnings_confirmation_gate.metric_logic: eps_gr_5y preferred; eps_gr_3y fallback when 5Y unavailable
earnings_confirmation_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — NaN EPS CAGR (after 5Y/3Y fallback chain) → 0 < 15 → gate fails. v1.1 change: eps_gr_5y (EPS per share) replaces pat_gr_3y (total PAT) to be dilution-adjusted.
earnings_confirmation_gate.rationale: Lynch Ch15: 'the earnings growth rate' — Lynch consistently uses per-share earnings, not total profit. A company with 25% revenue growth but issuing shares aggressively can show PAT growth while EPS is flat. Using EPS correctly penalizes dilutive growers. 5Y CAGR preferred for smoothing; 3Y fallback for newer listings. Threshold at 15% (5pp discount to revenue) allows for reinvestment costs while confirming the earnings story.

cash_verification_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(-1) for both — NaN FCF/yield → -1 > 0 → False. Missing cash data = cannot verify positive FCF → gate fails conservatively.
cash_verification_gate.rationale: Lynch Ch13 p196: 'Before you invest in a growth company, make sure that it's free cash flow — what's left after normal capital spending.' Earnings can be inflated by working capital manipulation; free cash flow is the ground truth. A company burning cash at the Fast Grower stage is a value trap, not a Fast Grower.

pillar_p_valuation_peg._book_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.13 p.183: 'A slightly more complicated formula enables us to compare growth rates to earnings... Less than a 1 is poor, and 1.5 is okay, but what you're really looking for is a 2 or better.' (This refers to Lynch's own ratio = (growth+div)/PE, which is the inverse of PEG.) Companion Ch.2 PEG Scale: '<0.5 Exceptional value; 0.5-0.75 Good value / Strong buy territory for fast growers; 0.75-1.0 Fair value.' Companion Ch.1 Fast Grower Buy Rule: 'PEG below 0.75 AND the story is intact.'
pillar_p_valuation_peg._display_label: GARP Entry Corridor: Positive PEG Ratio <= 0.75

peg_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — NaN PEG (no earnings or negative PE) → 999 >> 0.75 → gate fails. PEG must be positive to be meaningful.
peg_gate.rationale: Lynch pioneered the PEG ratio as the primary valuation tool for growth companies. The India companion documents: 'Every Indian tenbagger had a PEG below 0.75 at discovery. Page Industries 2008: PEG ~0.5. Eicher Motors 2005: PEG ~0.4. Bajaj Finance 2012: PEG ~0.6.' The positive PEG filter (peg > 0) excludes negative-PE stocks (losing money) and infinite-PE stocks where PEG is undefined. PEG <= 0.75 is the sweet spot where growth significantly outpaces the price paid for it.

pillar_d_pre_discovery._book_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.9 p.122: 'If you find a stock with little or no institutional ownership, you've found a potential winner. Find a company that no analyst has ever visited, or that no analyst would admit to knowing about, and you've got a double winner.' Ch.15 p.208: 'percentage of institutional ownership — the lower the better.' Companion Ch.3 Characteristic #4: 'FII+DII below 20% AND analyst coverage below 3 = pre-discovery.' Companion Ch.7 India Filter: Institutional Ownership FII+DII < 20%.
pillar_d_pre_discovery._display_label: Combined Institutional Weight: FII plus DII below the pre-discovery threshold

institutional_ownership_gate.fillna_strategy: fii_holdings fillna(50) AND dii_holdings fillna(50) — NaN FII or DII holdings → assume already discovered (conservatively 50% each) → combined 100 >= 20 → gate fails. v1.1 change: combined FII+DII < 20% replaces FII < 10% to capture all institutional investors.
institutional_ownership_gate.rationale: Lynch's insight: once large institutions discover a stock, their combined buying power drives multiples up and compresses future alpha. v1.1 correctly uses COMBINED institutional ownership (FII+DII < 20%) as Lynch specified in Ch15 'percentage of institutional ownership' — not just FII. A stock with 5% FII but 20% DII (total 25%) is already institutionally owned and should not pass the pre-discovery gate. v1.0 FII-only proxy was too lenient.
institutional_ownership_gate.v10_note: v1.0 used FII < 10% as a proxy. v1.1 corrects to (FII+DII) < 20% to match Lynch's actual criterion of 'institutional ownership' meaning all institutions.

pillar_f_fortress_owner._pillar: F — Fortress Balance Sheet & Owner Conviction
pillar_f_fortress_owner._book_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.13 p.185: 'One quick way to determine the financial strength of a company is to compare the equity to the debt on the right side of the balance sheet.' Companion Ch.8 Item 2: 'D/E below 0.25: Lynch's preferred level for fast growers.' Companion Ch.9 Screen 4: 'Promoter Holding > 45%.' Ch.15 p.208: 'insider buying is a positive sign.' Ch.15 p.213: 'Insiders are buying — I consider it a positive sign.' Companion Ch.3 Characteristic #10: 'Insiders are buying — Promoter open-market purchases are the strongest Lynch signal.'
pillar_f_fortress_owner._display_label: Skin In The Game Shield: D/E < 0.5 and Promoter Holdings >= 45% OR active buying

debt_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — NaN D/E → unknown leverage → 999 >= 0.5 → gate fails.
debt_gate.rationale: Lynch: 'Companies without debt don't go bankrupt.' High-growth strategies fail during macro contractions if they rely on expensive bank leverage. The screener gate (companion Ch.9) uses D/E < 0.5 for practical screening. Financial sector exempt — banks structurally carry high leverage as part of their business model.
debt_gate.threshold_note: Lynch's stated preference is D/E < 0.25 (companion Ch.8). The screener gate (companion Ch.9) uses D/E < 0.5 for practical screening. Gate is STRICT '<': exactly 0.5 FAILS (unlike Malik's <= 0.5).

promoter_gate.fillna_strategy: promoter_holdings fillna(0) — NaN promoter → 0 < 45 → fails level gate. change_promoter_1y fillna(-1) — NaN change → -1 > 0 → fails buying gate. Both gates must fail for Pillar F to fail promoter sub-condition.
promoter_gate.rationale: v1.1 extends the promoter gate with an OR condition for insider buying. Lynch Ch15 mentions insider buying TWICE (p208 and p213): 'insider buying is a positive sign.' A promoter actively increasing stake to 42% is more conviction-aligned than a passive 45% holder who is selling. The OR logic: promoter conviction can be demonstrated either by holding level (>= 45%) OR by actively accumulating (change > 0). A promoter with < 45% but ACTIVELY BUYING satisfies Lynch's insider-buying criterion.

financial_sector_exemption.behaviour: Financial companies (banks, NBFCs, insurance) are FULLY EXEMPT from the debt gate only. D/E > 1.0 is structurally normal for leveraged financial businesses. Promoter holding OR buying requirement still applies.

inventory_surge_disqualifier._description: Inventory surge vetos lynch_pass WITHOUT affecting lynch_score. A company can score 4/4 on all pillars but still fail lynch_pass if inventories are growing faster than sales.
inventory_surge_disqualifier._book_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.13 p.197: 'Keep an eye on inventories. If a retailer or a manufacturer is building up inventories, that's a first sign that things aren't going well. When inventories grow faster than sales, watch out.'

condition.fillna_strategy: inv_growth.notna() guard — NaN inventory growth = no data = disqualifier DOES NOT fire. Only triggers when inv_growth data is present AND clearly exceeds revenue growth by 20pp+.
condition.rationale: Lynch's earliest warning signal: inventories growing materially faster than revenue indicates demand weakness, overproduction, or channel stuffing. The 20pp margin above revenue growth filters noise (seasonal inventory builds) and only triggers on structural imbalance. This veto keeps lynch_score as a pure 4-pillar fundamental measure while preventing a clearly deteriorating inventory position from earning a full Lynch certification.

one_directional_implication.v11: lynch_pass == 1 → lynch_score == 4 (one-directional). lynch_score == 4 does NOT imply lynch_pass == 1 when inventory surge is active. A score=4 with pass=0 signals: all pillars strong BUT inventory accumulation risk present.

pass_flag_logic._logic: AND of all 4 materialized pillar flags: lynch_growth_velocity & lynch_valuation_peg & lynch_pre_discovery & lynch_fortress_owner AND NOT inventory_surge_disqualifier
pass_flag_logic._description: 1 = confirmed Peter Lynch Fast Grower tenbagger candidate: hyper-growth confirmed (EPS+FCF), PEG in sweet spot, pre-institutional discovery phase (FII+DII < 20%), fortress balance sheet with owner conviction, and no inventory accumulation red flag. 0 = one or more gates failed, or inventory surge disqualifier active.
pass_flag_logic._v11_note: v1.1 adds inventory_surge disqualifier: lynch_pass=0 even when lynch_score=4 if inv_growth > rev_gr_5y + 20pp. Score measures fundamental pillar quality; pass measures Lynch certification including the Ch13 inventory warning.

score_column_logic._description: Sum of all 4 pillar flags. Score=4 is necessary but NOT sufficient for lynch_pass=1 (v1.1 one-directional). Score=3 = one gate blocking. Enables near-miss ranking of Lynch Fast Grower compliance independent of the inventory disqualifier. A score=4 with pass=0 means all fundamentals are strong but inventory accumulation risk is present.
score_column_logic._v11_note: v1.0: Score=4 was identical to lynch_pass=1. v1.1: Score=4 only implies lynch_pass=1 when inventory surge disqualifier is NOT active. Direction: pass=1 always → score=4, but score=4 does NOT always → pass=1.
score_column_logic._use_case: Sort by lynch_score descending to rank companies approaching the full Lynch standard. A score of 3/4 blocking only on lynch_pre_discovery (already discovered by institutions) is a very different investment case from 3/4 blocking on lynch_valuation_peg (overpriced).

scoring_matrix.disqualifier_note: Inventory surge disqualifier affects lynch_pass only, not scoring_matrix weights or score_range.

vectorization_matrix.description: All 4 gates are pure vectorized Pandas/NumPy boolean masks. Zero loops, zero apply(), zero iterrows(). NaN strategy is conservative: missing data defaults to gate failure for all numeric gates.

nan_handling.rev_gr_5y: fillna(0) → 0 < 20 → gate fails (cannot verify Fast Grower status)
nan_handling.eps_gr_5y: fillna(0) after 5Y/3Y fallback chain → 0 < 15 → gate fails (v1.1: EPS per share replaces pat_gr_3y)
nan_handling.eps_gr_3y: fallback for eps_gr_5y; fillna(0) → 0 < 15 → gate fails if both unavailable
nan_handling.pat_gr_3y: v1.0 legacy column — NOT used in v1.1 Lynch scoring. Retained in NaN handling registry for reference.
nan_handling.free_cash_flow: fillna(-1) → -1 > 0 → False → cash gate fails (conservative: unknown FCF = cannot verify positive cash)
nan_handling.fcf_yield: fillna(-1) → -1 > 0 → False → cash gate fallback fails
nan_handling.peg: fillna(999) → 999 > 0.75 → gate fails (unknown PEG = unknown valuation)
nan_handling.fii_holdings: fillna(50) → 50 + 50 = 100 >= 20 → gate fails (assume already discovered if data missing)
nan_handling.debt_to_equity: fillna(999) → 999 >= 0.5 → gate fails (unknown leverage never safe)
nan_handling.change_promoter_1y: fillna(-1) → -1 > 0 → False → buying gate fails (NaN = not buying)
nan_handling.inv_growth: notna() guard → NaN = no data = disqualifier DOES NOT fire (safe default)

not_implementable.reason: Cannot categorize 'boringness' from financial ratio CSV. Requires human scuttlebutt observation.
not_implementable.chapter_reference: One Up on Wall Street Ch.9, Companion Ch.3

not_implementable.reason: StockScan CSV does not include analyst coverage count. FII+DII < 20% (v1.1) is the implementable proxy.

not_implementable.gate: Same-store sales growth >= 8% (sell trigger for retail Fast Growers)
not_implementable.reason: Same-store sales data (SSSG) is not disclosed in a standardized format in India — requires company-specific parsing of quarterly results.
not_implementable.chapter_reference: Companion Ch.10 Sell Fast Growers When

not_implementable.gate: Model replicability check (new-geography unit economics match original)
not_implementable.reason: Requires per-store or per-branch profitability data not available in aggregated CSV.

implemented_in_v11.status: IMPLEMENTED in v1.1 Pillar F as OR condition alongside promoter_holdings >= 45%.
implemented_in_v11.chapter_reference: Ch.15 p.208+213: 'insider buying is a positive sign.'

implemented_in_v11.gate: Inventory growing faster than sales (inventory surge disqualifier)
implemented_in_v11.status: IMPLEMENTED in v1.1 as inventory_surge_disqualifier: inv_growth > (rev_gr_5y + 20pp) vetos lynch_pass.
implemented_in_v11.chapter_reference: Ch.13 p.197: 'when inventories grow faster than sales, watch out.'

lynch_growth_velocity.interpretation: 1 = Revenue 5Y CAGR >= 20% AND EPS per share >= 15% (5Y preferred, 3Y fallback) AND positive FCF/yield; confirmed Fast Grower with dilution-adjusted earnings and cash verification.

lynch_valuation_peg.interpretation: 1 = PEG ratio is positive AND <= 0.75; stock is in Lynch's tenbagger sweet spot (growth significantly outpaces price paid).

lynch_pre_discovery.interpretation: 1 = FII+DII combined holdings < 20%; stock is in the pre-institutional-discovery phase. v1.1: combined threshold (vs v1.0 FII-only < 10%) to match Lynch's actual 'institutional ownership' criterion.

lynch_fortress_owner.interpretation: 1 = D/E < 0.5 (or financial sector) AND (promoter holdings >= 45% OR change_promoter_1y > 0); fortress balance sheet with owner conviction by level or active insider buying.

lynch_pass.interpretation: 1 = all 4 pillar gates cleared AND inventory surge disqualifier not active; confirmed Peter Lynch Fast Grower tenbagger candidate. v1.1: pass=1 implies score=4, but score=4 does NOT always imply pass=1 (inventory surge can veto).

lynch_score.interpretation: 0-4 count of pillar gates cleared (inventory surge excluded from score). Score=4 with pass=0 = all fundamentals strong but inventory accumulation risk present.

vs_canslim.focus: CAN SLIM (O'Neill): quarterly EPS acceleration + technical breakout + institutional accumulation. Lynch: fundamental long-term PEG + pre-institutional discovery. Opposite on institutional ownership — Lynch WANTS low FII+DII; CAN SLIM wants FII buying.
vs_canslim.key_difference: CAN SLIM buys after institutional discovery begins. Lynch buys BEFORE it.

vs_coffee_can.focus: Coffee Can (Mukherjea): 10-year ROCE sustained compounders. Lynch: 5-year Fast Grower momentum with PEG valuation gate.
vs_coffee_can.key_difference: Coffee Can holds forever regardless of valuation. Lynch has explicit PEG-based exit (sell when PEG > 1.5 AND decelerating).

vs_malik.focus: Malik: SSGR self-funding as primary structural filter + CFO/PAT cash quality. Lynch: hyper-growth velocity + pre-discovery timing + PEG valuation.
vs_malik.key_difference: A Lynch tenbagger can have SSGR < growth (funded by retained earnings + modest leverage) and still pass Lynch. It would fail Malik's SSGR gate. Lynch tolerates some debt if growth is high enough; Malik does not.

distinction_from_other_frameworks.ssgr_unique_in_malik: The SSGR self-funding gate is unique to Malik. Lynch has no equivalent signal.
distinction_from_other_frameworks.peg_unique_in_lynch: The PEG <= 0.75 valuation gate is unique to Lynch across all 32 frameworks. Lynch specifically developed and popularized the PEG ratio.
